query: What is a leave of absence and do I still have to pay fees during it?

You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the relevant authority whether you can receive family or student financial aid. - Insurance: Co-insurance with relatives or student self-insurance may not be possible. Please enquire directly with your insurance company.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: Reimbursement of the tuition fee Already paid tuition fee can be reimbursed for certain, precisley defined reasons. This is the case, for example, if your application for remission or leave of absence has been granted. Further examples: You have completed your studies before the end of the admission period or you have paid too much. Related Links: - [Reimbursement of tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) Related entries: - [Leave of absence](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30834&cHash=6c0061df445fb72ace014b66819ed159) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Remission of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31930&cHash=ec520748e744f3ee49dc41584e03d431) - Remission of the tuition fee Citizens of an EU/EEA country or equivalent non-EU/EEA citizens can apply for remission of the tuition fee provided that: - it has been submitted on time and in full and - there is a recognised reason for remission. The application for remission is granted, but you have already paid the tuition fee? Read the information on tuition fee reimbursement and note the application deadlines. Related Links: - [Remission](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Equal status with EU/EEA citizens with regard to the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31127&cHash=a9fb293a7c3370b27dd98f693d1ca082) - [Reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31959&cHash=7d2042e16b5c6049dc06cc8398971bfa) - Residence permit ("visa") Residence permits authorise persons from third countries to settle in Austria (for a limited period) and/or to work here. Please enquire about this topic at an Austrian representation authority (embassy, consulate) or the Municipal Department 35 (MA 35). A residence permit can also have an influence on the amount of

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
retrieved.text: u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already paid. - I am admitted to a continuing education programme at the Postgraduate Center and a bachelor/diploma or master programme. What do I have to keep in mind? If you are admitted at the Postgraduate and to a degree programme there are two options: 1. Only the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. No further payment or re-enrolment is necessary. You will be automatically re-enroled in your degree programme when the fee is received at the Postgraduate Center. 2. The tuition fee and the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you (due to your citizenship or because you have exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters) You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. In addition you have to pay the tuition fee (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: Leave of absence before the start of the semester
retrieved.text: evident from them. - Military service, training service or civilian service Proof: - confirmation by the military or civil service agency - Voluntary year of environmental protection, memorial, peace or social service abroad Proof: - confirmation by the agency or mediating authority - Temporary impairment in relation to a disability Proof: - disability card and - medical or comparable confirmation - Professional activities This refers to at least four weeks of significant impairment of the pursuit of the degree programme due to professional activity or further training and qualification measures due to professional activity. Proof: - [confirmation of the employer (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Beurlaubung/Formular_Dienststelle_EN.pdf) of a significant interference with your studies during at least four weeks due to your employment which exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügigkeitsgrenze") - in case of training and qualification measures: additionally, a confirmation of participation/registration by the training institute including number of lessons and course duration Please note: - Educational measures that are not related to a professional activity are not considered as reasons for a leave of absence. - Pay slips cannot be accepted as proof. Employment contracts are accepted in exceptional cases, provided they are not older than 12 weeks. - As proof of self-employment, a corresponding confirmation (tax book extract, cash register contract, etc.) in conjunction with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment can be accepted. - Settlement of issues with authorities

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: Leave of absence before the start of the semester
retrieved.text: older than 12 weeks. - As proof of self-employment, a corresponding confirmation (tax book extract, cash register contract, etc.) in conjunction with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment can be accepted. - Settlement of issues with authorities abroad This concerns a significant interference with your studies for at least four weeks due to the settlement of issues with authorities abroad. Proof: - summons, official order or other confirmation letter by an authority - Current situation in Ukraine Due to the current situation in Ukraine, students from Ukraine and the Russian Federation can apply for leave of absence in summer semester 2026 until 28 February. Write a short explanation for your prevention from studying in the online form in the Servicedesk.

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: you have to pay the Student's Union fee every semester within the admission period, even if you are exempt from paying the tuition fee. The Student's Union (ÖH) fee does not remain at the university but is transferred to the Austrian National Union of Students. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students federal body of Representatives](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en) - [Student’s Union of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Tolerance semesters](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31297&cHash=f0dee5ae0ea77af89fdfedaca07ca13b) - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - [Students’ Union (ÖH) insurance](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31953&cHash=ad6e71b25212dfc34b1314efc4ae3cb1) - [Payment period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32002&cHash=50174e9655aa9a11d1bb85ebc71fc50f) - Studienberechtigungsprüfung The Studienberechtigungsprüfung gives you the opportunity of admission to a degree programme if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. However, it is no general university entrance qualification. It is only valid for a certain group of degree programmes. Related Links: - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [General university entrance qualification](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31898&cHash=9a39467d6150693b5f0f6ba07ed49907) - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) The StudiesServiceCenter (in German: StudienServiceCenter, SSC) and the StudiesServiceUnit (in German: StudienServiceStelle, SSS) are central points of contact for questions about your degree programme. Each degree programme has a StudiesServiceCenter; the majority of degree programmes also have a StudiesServiceUnit, which informs you about your degree programme and the administration of your studies. On the websites of your SSC/SSS, you can find important information about your degree programme, relevant news and often forms to download. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter

retrieved.title: Equal status with EU/EEA citizens
retrieved.text: paying? If your application for equal status has been approved, the same rules apply to you as for students who are nationals of an EU/EEA country. This means that the duration of your studies affects the amount of tuition fee. Your first semester is the semester in which you were first admitted to a degree program. It doesn't matter when you were granted equal status. The semester count does not restart. - I have already paid the tuition fee and have now been granted equal status. Will I get my money back? Yes, a reimbursement of the difference in fees is possible. To claim it, you must submit an [application for reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/#c1105696). - If you are within the planned duration of studies and tolerance semesters, everything except the Students' Union fee can be refunded. - If you have exceeded the planned duration of studies and tolerance semester, you will only be refunded the difference. When applying for a reimbursement at the Service Desk, select the reimbursement reason "I have paid too much, so I am applying for a reimbursement of the difference". - Why do I have to submit the application again and again? If your residence permit has a limited period of validity, so does your equal status. You must therefore submit a new application when you extend your residence permit.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: Leave of absence during the semester
retrieved.text: A leave of absence during the semester is only possible if you can prove that one of the following reasons occurred and only became relevant after the start of the semester: / - Pregnancy Proof: - parent-child pass with calculated date of birth or medical confirmation and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Childcare Proof: - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the applicant and - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the child and - birth certificate of the child and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. Please note: - If you raise your own children, a leave of absence is possible until the children reach the age of legal majority. Both parents can be on leave at the same time. - Care for relatives Proof: - proof of kinship (birth/marriage certificate) and - notification of care level or medical confirmation regarding the need for care (no diagnosis required) and - statutory declaration from the applicant stating that the relative is being cared for by the applicant and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Medical condition that verifiably prevents progress in the degree programme Proof: - [medical confirmation (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Beurlaubung/Formular_medizinische_Bestaetigung_Beurlaubung_EN.pdf) confirming that you have been or will

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text: the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
reranked.text: Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the relevant authority whether you can receive family or student financial aid. - Insurance: Co-insurance with relatives or student self-insurance may not be possible. Please enquire directly with your insurance company.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: completing my degree programme before 31 October/31 March (date of the last examination). Do I have to pay? If you complete your degree programme in winter semester until 31 October, and you are not admitted to any other degree programme you do not need to pay for the winter semester. This also applies, if you have already completed the last examination but the result is only entered after 31 October. The important thing is that the date of the last examination is on 31 October at the latest. The same applies if you complete your degree programme until 31 March in summer semester. If you have already paid the tuition fee, you can submit an [application for reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). - My fee does not register as "received" seven days after paying. What can I do? If your fee is not registered as received seven days after you have paid, it is likely that an error in the payment process has occurred. Check your payment data (payment reference according to u:space, amount of the paid fee, ...). In case of an incorrect payment the bank cannot allocate the paid fee to your student account at the University of Vienna. Thus you cannot be re-enroled in your studies. 1. Your unique 12-digit payment reference is not correct, is missing or was entered wrongly (e.g. in the field "Verwendungszweck" instead of "Zahlungsreferenz"). If this

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: Reimbursement of the tuition fee Already paid tuition fee can be reimbursed for certain, precisley defined reasons. This is the case, for example, if your application for remission or leave of absence has been granted. Further examples: You have completed your studies before the end of the admission period or you have paid too much. Related Links: - [Reimbursement of tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) Related entries: - [Leave of absence](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30834&cHash=6c0061df445fb72ace014b66819ed159) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Remission of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31930&cHash=ec520748e744f3ee49dc41584e03d431) - Remission of the tuition fee Citizens of an EU/EEA country or equivalent non-EU/EEA citizens can apply for remission of the tuition fee provided that: - it has been submitted on time and in full and - there is a recognised reason for remission. The application for remission is granted, but you have already paid the tuition fee? Read the information on tuition fee reimbursement and note the application deadlines. Related Links: - [Remission](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Equal status with EU/EEA citizens with regard to the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31127&cHash=a9fb293a7c3370b27dd98f693d1ca082) - [Reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31959&cHash=7d2042e16b5c6049dc06cc8398971bfa) - Residence permit ("visa") Residence permits authorise persons from third countries to settle in Austria (for a limited period) and/or to work here. Please enquire about this topic at an Austrian representation authority (embassy, consulate) or the Municipal Department 35 (MA 35). A residence permit can also have an influence on the amount of

reranked.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
reranked.text: u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You are not able to or do not want to pay the remaining difference? Please contact us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/39/create/706). We can remit the part of the fee you have already paid. - I am admitted to a continuing education programme at the Postgraduate Center and a bachelor/diploma or master programme. What do I have to keep in mind? If you are admitted at the Postgraduate and to a degree programme there are two options: 1. Only the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. No further payment or re-enrolment is necessary. You will be automatically re-enroled in your degree programme when the fee is received at the Postgraduate Center. 2. The tuition fee and the Students' Union fee is prescribed for you (due to your citizenship or because you have exceeded the stipulated degree programme duration + tolerance semesters) You pay the Students' Union fee as part of the program fee at the Postgraduate Center. In addition you have to pay the tuition fee (without Students' Union fee) for your degree programme. - I am also studying at a university of applied sciences (Fachhochschule). How much do I have to pay? Universities of applied sciences ("Fachhochschulen" FH) forward the information that the Students' Union

heading_path: Leave of absence before the start of the semester
reranked.text: evident from them. - Military service, training service or civilian service Proof: - confirmation by the military or civil service agency - Voluntary year of environmental protection, memorial, peace or social service abroad Proof: - confirmation by the agency or mediating authority - Temporary impairment in relation to a disability Proof: - disability card and - medical or comparable confirmation - Professional activities This refers to at least four weeks of significant impairment of the pursuit of the degree programme due to professional activity or further training and qualification measures due to professional activity. Proof: - [confirmation of the employer (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Beurlaubung/Formular_Dienststelle_EN.pdf) of a significant interference with your studies during at least four weeks due to your employment which exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügigkeitsgrenze") - in case of training and qualification measures: additionally, a confirmation of participation/registration by the training institute including number of lessons and course duration Please note: - Educational measures that are not related to a professional activity are not considered as reasons for a leave of absence. - Pay slips cannot be accepted as proof. Employment contracts are accepted in exceptional cases, provided they are not older than 12 weeks. - As proof of self-employment, a corresponding confirmation (tax book extract, cash register contract, etc.) in conjunction with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment can be accepted. - Settlement of issues with authorities

Proof: - summons, official order or other confirmation letter by an authority - Current situation in Ukraine Due to the current situation in Ukraine, students from Ukraine and the Russian Federation can apply for leave of absence in summer semester 2026 until 28 February. The Student's Union (ÖH) fee does not remain at the university but is transferred to the Austrian National Union of Students. Related Links: - [Austrian National Union of Students federal body of Representatives](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en) - [Student’s Union of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/) Related entries: - [Austrian National Union of Students (ÖH)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31211&cHash=cc183cd54b32092671cf06b22d1ad46f) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Tolerance semesters](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31297&cHash=f0dee5ae0ea77af89fdfedaca07ca13b) - [Re-enrolment](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31949&cHash=3e7137171e154715f00818454de75f06) - [Students’ Union (ÖH) insurance](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31953&cHash=ad6e71b25212dfc34b1314efc4ae3cb1) - [Payment period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=32002&cHash=50174e9655aa9a11d1bb85ebc71fc50f) - Studienberechtigungsprüfung The Studienberechtigungsprüfung gives you the opportunity of admission to a degree programme if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. However, it is no general university entrance qualification. It is only valid for a certain group of degree programmes. Related Links: - [Studienberechtigungsprüfung](/en/admission/studying-without-secondary-school-leaving-certificate/) Related entries: - [General university entrance qualification](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31898&cHash=9a39467d6150693b5f0f6ba07ed49907) - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS) The StudiesServiceCenter (in German: StudienServiceCenter, SSC) and the StudiesServiceUnit (in German: StudienServiceStelle, SSS) are central points of contact for questions about your degree programme. Each degree programme has a StudiesServiceCenter; the majority of degree programmes also have a StudiesServiceUnit, which informs you about your degree programme and the administration of your studies. On the websites of your SSC/SSS, you can find important information about your degree programme, relevant news and often forms to download. Related Links: - [StudiesServiceCenter

reranked.title: Equal status with EU/EEA citizens
reranked.text: paying? If your application for equal status has been approved, the same rules apply to you as for students who are nationals of an EU/EEA country. This means that the duration of your studies affects the amount of tuition fee. Your first semester is the semester in which you were first admitted to a degree program. It doesn't matter when you were granted equal status. The semester count does not restart. - I have already paid the tuition fee and have now been granted equal status. Will I get my money back? Yes, a reimbursement of the difference in fees is possible. To claim it, you must submit an [application for reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/#c1105696). - If you are within the planned duration of studies and tolerance semesters, everything except the Students' Union fee can be refunded. - If you have exceeded the planned duration of studies and tolerance semester, you will only be refunded the difference. When applying for a reimbursement at the Service Desk, select the reimbursement reason "I have paid too much, so I am applying for a reimbursement of the difference". - Why do I have to submit the application again and again? If your residence permit has a limited period of validity, so does your equal status. You must therefore submit a new application when you extend your residence permit.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: Leave of absence during the semester
reranked.text: A leave of absence during the semester is only possible if you can prove that one of the following reasons occurred and only became relevant after the start of the semester: / - Pregnancy Proof: - parent-child pass with calculated date of birth or medical confirmation and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Childcare Proof: - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the applicant and - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the child and - birth certificate of the child and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. Please note: - If you raise your own children, a leave of absence is possible until the children reach the age of legal majority. Both parents can be on leave at the same time. - Care for relatives Proof: - proof of kinship (birth/marriage certificate) and - notification of care level or medical confirmation regarding the need for care (no diagnosis required) and - statutory declaration from the applicant stating that the relative is being cared for by the applicant and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Medical condition that verifiably prevents progress in the degree programme Proof: - [medical confirmation (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Beurlaubung/Formular_medizinische_Bestaetigung_Beurlaubung_EN.pdf) confirming that you have been or will

A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

selected.title: Leave of absence
selected.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. selected.title: Leave of absence
selected.text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

selected.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
selected.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

[5] Leave of absence — What do you need to consider?
If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

[6] Leave of absence — FAQ
apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Leave of absence — What do you need to consider?
absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending courses is - possible until the end of the [admission period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/) if you apply for a leave of absence during the admission period and were already admitted in the previous semester. - possible until the date of application if you apply for leave of absence after the end of the admission period. You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester

[2] Leave of absence — FAQ
after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

[3] Leave of absence — What do you need to consider?
fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

Question: What is a leave of absence and do I still have to pay fees during it?

generate.raw_response: A leave of absence is a period where you temporarily stop studying [1][5]. During this time, you are exempt from paying the tuition fee [1][2], but you still have to pay the Students' Union fee to avoid automatic closure of your studies [1][2].

answer: A leave of absence is a period where you temporarily stop studying [1][5]. During this time, you are exempt from paying the tuition fee [1][2], but you still have to pay the Students' Union fee to avoid automatic closure of your studies [1][2].
